title: GoFundMe launched for Mass. family after teen dies in crash from overpass
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Travis Teixeira, 18, from Ashburnham, died after his vehicle fell from a Route 2 overpass in Leominster.
A GoFundMe campaign has raised over $70,000 to support Teixeira's family with funeral expenses and other financial needs.
Massachusetts State Police reported responding to the crash; details about other potential occupants remain undisclosed.

title: ‘Stay strong’: David Ortiz’s defiant declaration in the wake of tragedy still resonates with Boston
author: Chad Finn
bullets: David Ortiz delivered a defining speech at Fenway Park after the Boston Marathon bombings on April 20, 2013.
Ortiz's words focused on unity and resilience, thanking city officials and asserting, 'This is our [expletive] city.'
Ortiz's speech took place during a pregame ceremony five days after the tragic events that left Boston in mourning.

title: Holy Cross student missing after canoe capsizes in Connecticut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Holy Cross student Dominick Tucci, 20, is missing after a canoe capsized on Lake Hayward in East Haddam, Connecticut.
A fellow student in the canoe was rescued and hospitalized, while search efforts for Tucci continue with police involvement.
Holy Cross provided counseling to students and is in contact with law enforcement during the search for Tucci.

title: The unsolved murder of Kendric Price, a beloved youth mentor gunned down on his childhood street in Dorchester
author: Emily Sweeney, The Boston Globe
bullets: Kendric Price, a 32-year-old youth mentor, was fatally shot on Greenwood Street in Dorchester in March 2019.
Price was involved in mentoring youth through his nonprofit Big Business Network and as a basketball coach.
Boston police continue to investigate the unsolved murder, urging anyone with information to contact homicide detectives.

title: The basement of this $4.7 m Lynnfield home known for its holiday decor is like a private club
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The 8,190-square-foot Lynnfield home listed for $4.7 million features a basement resembling a private club.
Basement amenities include an eight-person bar, vintage cash register, private screening room, and kids' playroom.
Outside, the property is known for its elaborate holiday decorations, attracting thousands during Halloween.

title: Patriots reportedly set to release QB Joshua Dobbs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots are reportedly set to release QB Joshua Dobbs, who signed a two-year deal in 2025.
Dobbs, 31, served as backup to Drake Maye and completed 7 out of 10 passes in four games last season.
Tommy DeVito's return on a two-year deal with guaranteed money may impact Dobbs's release.

title: Tracy McGrady questions whether Celtics are ‘still on same the page’ following Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tracy McGrady questions Celtics' cohesion post-Jayson Tatum's return after a 10-month recovery from a ruptured Achilles’ tendon.
Celtics' shooting percentages have declined since Tatum's return, with performance struggles against Memphis and Minnesota highlighted.
Carmelo Anthony suggests Tatum play closer to the basket to improve efficiency, despite his heavy reliance on 3-point attempts.

title: One New Englander is in this year’s class at Boston’s Folk Americana Roots Hall of Fame: Tom Rush
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Tom Rush, a Portsmouth, N.H. native, is being inducted into the Folk Americana Roots Hall of Fame on March 24.
Rush is the only New Englander among the 2026 inductees, including Neil Young, Judy Collins, and Jackson Browne.
The induction ceremony will be held in Boston and feature performances by Rush, Judy Collins, and Tom Paxton.

title: Jaylen Brown sheds light on 1-on-1 meeting with Jayson Tatum ahead of playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum held a meeting to strengthen communication before Tatum's return from a 10-month Achilles injury.
The meeting aimed to set a positive tone for the Boston Celtics as they prepare for the upcoming playoffs in April.
Brown and Tatum discussed maintaining focus amid external pressures, emphasizing teamwork for a successful playoff performance.

title: Livestream: Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial gets underway
author: John Waller
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, was shot by a colleague; her trial begins this week.
Fitzsimmons has pleaded not guilty to assault with a dangerous weapon after a home incident.
The trial is being livestreamed by NBC10 Boston and is underway this week.
